title: Cosmetic Oil on Cotton
timeEstimate: 20–45 minutes; powder preload + Dawn × 2 (silicone barrier), no dryer until clear
stainChemistry: Cosmetic oils (face serums, body oils, massage oils, hair oils) are usually a mix of botanical oils, silicones (dimethicone, cyclomethicone), and essential oils. The silicone is the catch — it's slick and water-repellent, so most household soaps can't get through it on the first pass. Standard cooking-oil chemistry (Dawn pretreat) works, but cotton typically needs 2 Dawn passes for silicone-heavy products. Cotton tolerates the full toolkit (Dawn, hot wash AFTER chemistry, 3% peroxide on whites, bounded chlorine on solid white cotton), but the order matters: powder preload BEFORE water, Dawn × 2 BEFORE any hot wash. The recurring trap is hot-drying residual oil — heat polymerizes the oil into the cotton and leaves a permanent yellow shadow.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload (cornstarch / baking soda) pulls bulk oil up before water can spread it. Generous Dawn dish soap breaks through the silicone barrier and emulsifies the botanical oils. A second Dawn pass clears residual silicone — silicone-coated stains resist single-pass cleaning because they re-deposit from wash water. Cool wash + AIR-DRY locks nothing. The honest cap: cosmetic oil that's been hot-dried for weeks sometimes leaves a permanent yellow shadow — set realistic expectations.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): cosmetic oils combine triglyceride botanical oils + silicones (dimethicone, cyclomethicone) + essential oils. Silicones are hydrophobic and form a water-repellent film. Pro uses POG / petroleum ether for silicone phase + amyl acetate for stubborn film + NSD + 3% H₂O₂ for residual chromophore.
defaultAssumption: Face serum / body oil / massage oil drip on a cotton shirt, sheet, or pillowcase. Fresh = high-confidence DIY with the powder + Dawn × 2 sequence. Hot-dried weeks-old yellowing = honest cap; pro is the right call.

spottingProtocol.technique: Pull bulk oil up before water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the oil spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 20–30 min absorb. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without any water — water-first spreads cosmetic oil into a wider ring.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Cotton tolerates warm Dawn — that helps the oil release. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: Test Oily Feel — Repeat Dawn If Yes
spottingProtocol.instruction: Touch with a clean fingertip. Slick or oily feel = repeat Dawn. Silicone-heavy serums typically need 2 Dawn passes — silicone re-deposits from wash water unless fully removed first.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, hot water (per care label)
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hot wash per care label. Safe at this point — chemistry has cleared the oil. Cotton tolerates hot up to 200°F. Inspect before dryer.
AIR-DRY first time. Inspect in daylight. Faint yellow shadow = repeat the Dawn pass. NEVER hot dryer with residual oil — locks it into cotton permanently.

steps: 1. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
2. Generous Dawn pretreat, warm, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
3. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains (silicone re-deposit).
4. Hot wash per care label — safe AFTER Dawn × 2.
5. AIR-DRY first time, inspect, repeat Dawn if yellow shadow remains.
products: Cool to warm water for chemistry, hot for wash
professionalProtocol.temperature: warm Dawn dwell + hot wash AFTER
warnings: Powder preload BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil
Dawn × 2 typical for silicone-heavy cosmetic oils
NEVER hot dryer with residual oil — polymerizes into cotton permanently
Apply Dawn directly, no water dilution before brush
Honest cap: hot-dried weeks-old yellowing sometimes plateaus — pro escalation

homeSolutions.instruction: Cosmetic oils on cotton are usually botanical oils + silicone + essential oils. The silicone is the catch — it's slick and water-repellent, so Dawn often needs two passes to break through. The order: powder preload → Dawn → repeat Dawn if oily → hot wash → air-dry first time. Two hard rules: powder BEFORE water (water-first spreads oil into a wider ring), and NO hot dryer until cleared (locks residual oil into cotton permanently).

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda — 20 to 30 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 20 to 30 minutes. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without any water.
Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly to the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Cotton tolerates warm (warm, not hot) water — helps the oil release. Damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Test for Oily Feel — Repeat If Slick
homeSolutions.instruction: Touch the spot with a clean fingertip. Still feels slick or oily? Repeat the Dawn step. Silicone-heavy serums typically need 2 Dawn passes — without the second pass, the silicone re-deposits from wash water and the stain doesn't fully clear.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash — Safe AFTER Two Dawn Passes
homeSolutions.instruction: Hot machine wash per care label. Safe at this point because the oil and silicone are gone — heat is finishing-side, not setting-side. Cotton tolerates hot up to 200°F.

homeSolutions.agent: AIR-DRY First Time, Inspect Before Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: AIR-DRY the first time. Inspect in daylight. Faint yellow shadow remaining? Repeat the Dawn pass before any heat. NEVER put residual oil in the hot dryer — it polymerizes into the cotton permanently and the yellow becomes a permanent shadow.

steps: 1. Powder BEFORE water. Dawn × 2 for silicone barrier. NO hot dryer until cleared.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, warm, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
4. Repeat Dawn pass if oily feel remains.
5. Hot wash per care label — safe AFTER Dawn × 2.
6. AIR-DRY first time, inspect, repeat Dawn if yellow shadow remains.
products: Warm water for chemistry, hot for wash
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot dryer was used on residual oil for weeks (often permanent yellow shadow). Stop if cosmetic oil sat through multiple wash cycles already (silicone re-deposit may be locked in). Stop after 3 home cycles plateau.
warnings: NEVER hot dryer with residual oil — polymerizes into cotton
Powder BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil into a wider ring
Dawn × 2 typical for silicone-heavy cosmetic oils
Honest cap: hot-dried weeks-old yellowing sometimes plateaus

neverDo: Never hot dryer with residual oil — polymerizes into cotton permanently
Never water-first on oil stains — spreads them
Never skip the second Dawn pass on silicone-heavy serums
Never chlorine bleach on dyed cotton
fiberSensitivities: White cotton: tolerates Dawn × 2 + hot wash + 3% peroxide if yellow shadow remains
Colored cotton: hidden test before any peroxide
Tolerates hot wash up to 200°F AFTER chemistry

materialWarnings: Powder before water — water-first spreads cosmetic oil
No hot dryer with residual oil — permanent yellow shadow
Hot wash is finishing-side AFTER chemistry

escalation.when: Hot dryer used on residual oil for weeks (often permanent yellow shadow). Cosmetic oil through multiple wash cycles with silicone re-deposit. 3 home cycles plateau. Specialty / dry-clean-only cotton blend.
escalation.whatToTell: "Cosmetic oil on cotton, [silicone-heavy serum / body oil / hair oil / massage oil], age [fresh / hours / weeks of buildup], home-treated with [cornstarch preload + Dawn × 2 + hot wash + air-dry]. No hot dryer applied. [Residual yellow shadow / silicone re-deposit suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with silicone-spotting capability (POG / petroleum ether)

contextQuestions: What kind of cosmetic oil — face serum, body oil, hair oil, massage oil, primer?
Silicone-heavy product (smooth slick feel) or pure botanical oil?
Fresh, hours old, or aged yellow shadow?
Has the cotton been hot-washed or hot-dried with the residue?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Silicone content is the entire challenge — most cosmetic oils now contain silicone for the slip / glide feel, and silicone resists single-pass cleaning. Dawn × 2 is the working unlock. Pure botanical oils (jojoba, argan, marula, coconut) without silicone are easier — single Dawn pass usually sufficient. Hair oils with heavy silicone are the hardest. Hot-dried residue is the classic yellow-shadow plateau.
